---
title: "Advanced `git`"
subtitle: "Block 3.2: git-bug"
---

# `git-bug` 🐛

## What is `git-bug`?

[`git-bug`](https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug) is a **distributed, offline-first bug tracker** that is embedded directly in your `git` repository.

- Issue tracking without a central server
- Bugs are stored as `git` objects alongside your code
- Works completely offline ✈️
- No extra accounts or services needed

## How does it work?

`git-bug` uses `git`'s own object model to store bugs.

- Bugs are stored in a special `git` namespace (`refs/bugs/`)
- Not regular commits, so they don't pollute your history
- Pushed and pulled together with your code
- Edits are tracked and can be merged like code

## Key Features 🌟

- Full bug lifecycle management
  - Open, comment, label, close bugs
- Works fully offline
- Built-in bridges to sync with external trackers
  - GitHub Issues, GitLab Issues, Jira, ...
- Multiple interfaces available
  - Command line, terminal UI (TUI), and web UI

## Interfaces

```bash
# Command line
git bug add
git bug ls
git bug show <id>
git bug comment add <id>

# Terminal UI
git bug termui

# Web UI
git bug webui
```

## Bridges to GitHub / GitLab 🌉

`git-bug` can sync issues with GitHub and GitLab.

```bash
# Configure a GitHub bridge
git bug bridge configure

# Import issues from GitHub
git bug bridge pull

# Push local bugs to GitHub
git bug bridge push
```

## Why `git-bug`?

:::{.nonincremental}

| | GitHub Issues | `git-bug` |
|---|---|---|
| Offline | ❌ | ✅ |
| Embedded in repo | ❌ | ✅ |
| Central server | Required | Optional |
| Sync with GitHub | ✅ | ✅ (via bridge) |

:::

## Installation

```bash
# Via go install
go install github.com/git-bug/git-bug@latest

# Or download a pre-built binary from:
# https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug/releases
```

Alternatively, package managers like `brew`, `nix`, `scoop`, etc.

## Further Reading

- Repository & Documentation: <https://github.com/git-bug/git-bug>

## *End of Block* 🎉 {background-color="black" background-opacity="0.2" background-image="images/backgrounds/kaiser.jpg"}

:::{.r-fit-text}
Any Questions?
:::

[[🏡 Back to Overview]](./index.html)

[[⏩️ Next Block]](./4.1-advanced-features.html)
